title: Web Application Vulnerability Scanner
description_html: In my Web Application Vulnerability Scanner project, I coordinated a three-person team and helped build a modular security testing web app using Python and Flask (deployed on Render). The tool accepts a target URL and runs a set of automated checks to identify common web security issues, including SQL Injection (SQLi), Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) (reflected, stored, and DOM indicators), security headers and related information leakage, cookie security attributes, SSL/TLS configuration checks (including a Heartbleed test when applicable), and network findings such as open ports and HTTP method exposure. The scan controller runs these modules concurrently using multithreading, and users can choose between a basic or deep scan (with deeper network testing such as directory traversal attempts). Results are displayed in a clean web interface and can be exported as a downloadable PDF report with findings organized by category and paired with remediation guidance.

title: Hybrid Intrusion Detection System
overlay: Hybrid IDS with Bi-LSTM, LightGBM & Rule-Based Logic
image_alt: TensorFlow icon representing the IDS machine-learning pipeline
description_html: In my Hybrid Intrusion Detection System project, I designed an ML-based IDS pipeline that combines Bi-LSTM temporal flow modeling, LightGBM tabular classification, and a rule-based logic layer for network traffic classification. I preprocessed and harmonized NSL-KDD, CIC-IDS-2017, and UNSW-NB15 using imputation, encoding, scaling, and sliding-window generation. I also tuned confidence thresholds to separate Normal, Known Attack, Suspicious, and Zero-Day-style traffic labels, and used LIME and SHAP to interpret model decisions and map important features into security-relevant categories.

title: Intrusion Detection, SIEM, and Network Security Labs
overlay: Suricata, OSSEC, ELK, Wireshark & Packet Analysis
image_alt: Wireshark icon representing packet analysis and network detection work
description_html: In my Intrusion Detection, SIEM, and Network Security Labs, I configured and analyzed network- and host-based detection workflows using Suricata, OSSEC, ELK/Kibana, Wireshark, tcpdump, and Nmap. The work included writing and reviewing Suricata rules, monitoring authentication and file-integrity events, analyzing PCAPs, reviewing DNS and TCP behavior, and inspecting suspicious scan and exploit-style traffic. This project strengthened my ability to connect packet-level evidence, host alerts, and SIEM-style views into a clearer detection workflow.

title: Multithreaded HTTP Client/Server
overlay: Python Sockets, TCP, HTTP Parsing & Concurrent Clients
image_alt: Python icon representing the socket-based HTTP client and server
description_html: In my Multithreaded HTTP Client/Server project, I implemented a Python socket-based client and server to practice TCP networking and HTTP-style communication. The server handles multiple clients using multithreading, parses simple request formats, supports GET/PUT-style behavior, and manages file-transfer style responses. I focused on understanding how application-layer requests sit on top of TCP connections, how concurrent clients are handled, and how protocol-level debugging helps identify networking issues.

title: Kronos Log Parser
overlay: C++ Log Parsing, Regex, Timestamp Handling & Reports
image_alt: C++ icon representing the Kronos log parser implementation
description_html: In my Kronos Log Parser project, I built a C++ program that reads raw log-style input, extracts useful fields, organizes records, and produces formatted output. The implementation uses parsing logic, regular expressions, timestamp handling, sorting, and report generation. I included this because it connects well with security operations work: practical SOC and detection workflows often depend on being able to parse, clean, and reason about log data accurately.

title: Fake News Detection with NLP and Machine Learning
overlay: TF-IDF, Logistic Regression, Random Forest & Evaluation
image_alt: scikit-learn icon representing the NLP classification pipeline
description_html: In the Fake News Detection project, I worked on an NLP and machine-learning pipeline for classifying news text. My contribution focused on text preprocessing, TF-IDF feature representation, visualization support, model evaluation, and comparison of classifiers such as Logistic Regression and Random Forest. The project helped me connect NLP feature engineering with practical model evaluation and communication of classification results.

title: Wearable Data for Recovery and Stress Monitoring
overlay: Wearable Stress Monitoring with Transformer + HMM
image_alt: Wearable stress and recovery monitoring charts
description_html: In the Wearable Data for Recovery and Stress Monitoring project, we worked with the LifeSnaps Fitbit dataset by merging wearable activity data with stress and affect survey scores. Performed data preprocessing including handling missing values, normalization, and feature selection to prepare time-series inputs for modeling. To analyze stress patterns over time, we implemented a Transformer-based model (from scratch using numpy) for temporal stress classification and a Hidden Markov Model (HMM) to capture latent stress and recovery state transitions, allowing comparison between deep learning and probabilistic sequence modeling approaches.

title: Trading Database Schema
overlay: Relational Schema Design, SQL Joins, Constraints & Triggers
image_alt: Oracle icon representing SQL and relational database work
description_html: In my Trading Database Schema project, I designed a relational database structure for a trading-style system. The work included identifying entities and relationships, creating tables, defining constraints, writing joins, using generated values, implementing triggers, and preparing CRUD-style SQL queries. I focused on keeping the schema organized, enforcing data integrity, and writing SQL logic that supports realistic application behavior. This project strengthened my understanding of relational modeling, normalization, query design, and backend data organization.

title: Personal Portfolio Website
overlay: React/Vite Portfolio with Project Filtering & Contact API
description_html: I designed and developed my Personal Portfolio Website as a full-stack web application to showcase my projects, technical skills, and academic background. The frontend is built using React, HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, with an emphasis on responsive design, smooth animations, and an intuitive user interface. The backend is implemented as a RESTful API using Node.js and Express, providing functionality for the contact form by securely handling user input and sending emails via a third-party email service. The frontend is deployed on GitHub Pages, while the backend is deployed on Render, demonstrating end-to-end deployment, environment configuration, and client–server integration.

title: Scrabble Game using JavaScript and jQuery
overlay: Interactive Scrabble Game with JS, jQuery & Scoring Logic
description_html: This one-line version of the Scrabble game is a streamlined implementation of the classic word game. This version focuses on delivering the core gameplay experience in a concise format, allowing players to form words from a given set of letters to score points based on the value of each letter. The game features a word validation system, a scoring mechanism, and a user-friendly interface for easy interaction. I designed this using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and jQuery, combining these technologies to create a responsive and interactive web application. HTML structures the content, CSS provides the styling, JavaScript handles the game logic, and jQuery simplifies DOM manipulation and enhances the user experience.

title: Sokoban Game using C++
overlay: C++ Puzzle Game with SFML Rendering
description_html: Sokoban Game is a classic puzzle video game in which the player pushes boxes or crates within a warehouse setting, trying to get them to designated storage locations. I implemented this using C++ which included logic for player movements, box interactions, and collision detection, and also making use of SFML for rendering graphics. The Sokoban class manages the game state, player position, and level data, while methods like movePlayer and isWon handle game mechanics and victory conditions. The game employs C++ standard libraries and smart pointers for efficient data management and memory use.

title: Cozmo Robot Navigation & Monte Carlo Localization
overlay: Monte Carlo Localization with Cozmo Robot using Python
description_html: In my Monte Carlo Localization (particle filter localization) project with Cozmo Robot, I worked as a team, implementing a probabilistic algorithm using Python to enable the robot to determine its position within a given environment/arena. The algorithm uses a set of particles to represent possible positions of the robot. Each particle is weighted based on sensor measurements compared to a pre-defined map, allowing the robot to estimate its position with increasing accuracy over time. Main elements of the implementation included sensor data integration, particle weight calculation, resampling techniques, and efficient handling of localization updates to ensure real-time performance.

title: Checkers Game using C++
description_html: Checkers is a game of two players played on a board. Each player in this game tries to eliminate all pieces belonging to his or her enemy and prevent those pieces from moving at all by utilizing their own pieces. I implemented this game using C++ that can be played in a terminal with the necessary SFML and C++ libraries. I utilized object-oriented programming principles to manage game states, validate moves, and handle the promotion of pieces to kings. The game supports a graphical user interface using the SFML library, enhancing the user experience with interactive visuals and gameplay mechanics.

title: Interactive Multiplication Table Generator
description_html: I designed an Interactive Multiplication Table Generator using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to provide a dynamic and user-friendly experience. This tool allows users to input a range of numbers to generate a multiplication table dynamically, updating in real-time as inputs are modified. The user interface is designed with simplicity and responsiveness in mind, ensuring that the multiplication tables are easy to read and interact with on various devices.

title: freeCodeCamp Magazine
description_html:
For this project, I developed a responsive online Magazine using HTML5, CSS3, and JavaScript, following the design principles and guidelines provided by freeCodeCamp as part of a Responsive Web Design Certificate Course by freeCodeCamp. This is a responsive design to ensure optimal viewing across various devices and screen sizes.

My Certificate for the course can be found here.
